core_payload.content:
# Key Agents Planning Lane

## 1. Boundary
Key Agents Planning Lane owns the temporal compression workers that turn long-horizon intent into grounded plans. This sub-hub exists so the planning swarm stays visible as its own lane instead of being flattened into the broader agent roster.

## 2. What Belongs Here
This sub-hub groups `daily-planner-agent`, `roadmap-planner-agent`, and `planning-horizon-engine`. Together they define day-level planning, roadmap shaping, and horizon compression from long-range intent into bounded execution structure.

## 3. What Does Not Belong Here
This sub-hub does not own conversational partnership, mining, maintenance execution, validation authority, or background swarm maintenance. Those belong to `user-lane`, `mining-lane`, `stewardship-lane`, `governance-lane`, and `swarm-lane`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Key Agents Planning Lane groups the temporal compression workers of N1Hub: daily planning, roadmap planning, and planning-horizon compression. It keeps planning intelligence distinct from mining, governance, and background swarm behavior. This matters because the system needs one visible lane that translates long-horizon intent into day-level sequencing, roadmap pressure, and bounded planning artifacts without mixing time-shaping work into discovery, critique, autonomous maintenance routines, reactive runtime firefighting, or avoidable schedule churn today.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted as part of key-agents hub decomposition to reduce parent link density from 28 outbound to ≤9.
